title: Northern Farmer (Old Style)
content:
Wheer ï¿½asta beï¿½n saw long and meï¿½ ligginï¿½ ï¿½ere aloï¿½n?
Noorse? thoort nowt oï¿½ a noorse: whoy, doctorï¿½s abeï¿½n anï¿½ agoï¿½n:
Says that I moï¿½nt ï¿½a naw moor aï¿½le: but I beï¿½nt a fool:
Git ma my aï¿½le, fur I beï¿½nt a-gooinï¿½ to breï¿½k my rule.

II.
Doctors, they knaws nowt, for a says whatï¿½s nawways true:
Naw soort oï¿½ koind oï¿½ use to saï¿½y the things that a do.
Iï¿½ve ï¿½ed my point oï¿½ aï¿½le ivry noight sinï¿½ I beï¿½n ï¿½ere,
Anï¿½ Iï¿½ve ï¿½ed my quart ivry market-noight for foorty year.

III.
Parsonï¿½s a beï¿½n loikewoise, anï¿½ a sittinï¿½ ere oï¿½ my bed.
ï¿½The amoightyï¿½s a taï¿½kin oï¿½ you to ï¿½issï¿½n, my friend,ï¿½ a said,
Anï¿½ a towd ma my sins, anï¿½s toithe were due, anï¿½ I gied it in hond;
I done my duty by un, as I ï¿½a done by the lond.

IV.
Larnï¿½d a maï¿½ beï¿½. I reckons I ï¿½annot sa mooch to larn.
But a cast oop, thot a did, ï¿½boot Bessy Marrisï¿½s barn.
Thaw a knaws I hallus voï¿½ted wiï¿½ Squoire anï¿½ choorch an staï¿½te,
Anï¿½ iï¿½ the woost oï¿½ toimes I wur niver agin the raï¿½te.

V.
Anï¿½ I hallus comed to ï¿½s choorch afoor moy Sally wur deï¿½d,
Anï¿½ ï¿½eï¿½rd ï¿½um a bumminï¿½ awaï¿½y loike a buzzard-clock ower my ï¿½eï¿½d,
Anï¿½ I niver knawï¿½d whot a meanï¿½d but I thowt a ï¿½ad summut to saï¿½y,
An I thowt a said whot a owt to ï¿½a said anï¿½ I comed awaï¿½y.

VI.
Bessy Marrisï¿½s barn! tha knaws she laï¿½id it to meï¿½.
Mowt ï¿½a beï¿½n, mayhap, for she wur a bad un, sheï¿½.
ï¿½Siver, I kep un, I kep un, my lass, tha mun understond;
I done my duty by un as I ï¿½a done boy the lond.

VII.
But Parson a comes anï¿½ a goï¿½s, anï¿½ a says it easy anï¿½ freeï¿½
ï¿½The amoightyï¿½s a taï¿½kin oï¿½ you to ï¿½issen, my friend,ï¿½ says ï¿½eï¿½.
I weï¿½nt saï¿½y men be loiars, thof summun said it in ï¿½aï¿½ste:
But a reï¿½ds wonn sarmin a weeï¿½k, anï¿½ I ï¿½a stubbï¿½d Thornaby waï¿½ste.

VIII.
Dï¿½ya moind the waï¿½ste, my lass? naw, naw, tha was not born then;
Theer wur a boggle in it, I often ï¿½eï¿½rd ï¿½um mysen;
Moï¿½st loike a butter-bump, for I ï¿½eï¿½rd ï¿½um about anï¿½ about,
But I stubbï¿½d un oop wiï¿½ the lot, anï¿½ raï¿½ved an rembled ï¿½um out.

IX.
Keï¿½perï¿½s it wur; foï¿½ they fun ï¿½um theer a laï¿½id on ï¿½is faï¿½ce
Down iï¿½ the woild ï¿½enemies afoor I coomï¿½d to the plaï¿½ce.
Noï¿½ks or Thimblebyï¿½toï¿½ner ï¿½ed shot un as deï¿½d as a naï¿½il.
Noï¿½ks wur ï¿½angï¿½d for it oop at ï¿½soizeï¿½but git ma my aï¿½le.

X.
Dubbut looï¿½k at the waï¿½ste: theer warnï¿½t not feeï¿½d for a cow:
Nowt at all but bracken anï¿½ fuzz, anï¿½ looï¿½k at it nowï¿½
Warnï¿½t worth nowt a haï¿½cre, anï¿½ now theerï¿½s lots oï¿½ feeï¿½d,
Fourscoor yows upon it anï¿½ some on it down iï¿½ seeï¿½d.

XI.
Nobbut a bit on itï¿½s left, anï¿½ I meï¿½nï¿½d to ï¿½a stubbï¿½d it at fall,
Done it ta-year I meï¿½nï¿½d, anï¿½ runnï¿½d plow thruff it anï¿½ all,
If godamoighty anï¿½ parson ï¿½ud nobbut let ma aloï¿½n,
Meï¿½, wiï¿½ haï¿½te hoonderd haï¿½cre oï¿½ Squoireï¿½s, anï¿½ lond oï¿½ my oï¿½n.

XII.
Do godamoighty knaw what aï¿½s doing a-taï¿½kinï¿½ oï¿½ meï¿½?
I beï¿½nt wonn as saws ï¿½ere a beï¿½n anï¿½ yonder a peï¿½;
Anï¿½ Squoire ï¿½ull be sa mad anï¿½ allï¿½aï¿½ dear aï¿½ dear!
And I ï¿½a managed for Squoire coom Michaelmas thutty year.

XIII.
A mowt ï¿½a taï¿½en owd Joï¿½nes, as ï¿½ant a ï¿½aï¿½poth oï¿½ sense,
Or a mowt aï¿½ taï¿½en young Robinsï¿½a niver mended a fence:
But godamoighty a moost taï¿½ke meï¿½ anï¿½ taï¿½ke ma now
Wi aï¿½f the cows to cauve anï¿½ Thornaby hoï¿½lms to plow!

XIV.
Loook ï¿½ow quoloty smoiles when they sees ma a passinï¿½ by,
Says to thessï¿½n naw doot ï¿½what a mon a beï¿½ sewer-loy!ï¿½
For they knaws what I beï¿½n to Squoire sin fust a coomï¿½d to the ï¿½All;
I done moy duty by Squoire anï¿½ I done my duty boy all.

XV.
Squoireï¿½s in Lunnon, anï¿½ summun I reckons ï¿½ull ï¿½a to wroite,
For whoï¿½s to howd the lond ater meï¿½ thot muddles ma quoit;
Sartin-sewer I beï¿½, thot a weï¿½nt niver give it to Joï¿½nes,
Naw, nor a moï¿½nt to Robinsï¿½a niver rembles the stoï¿½ns.

XVI.
But summun ï¿½ull come ater meï¿½ mayhap wiï¿½ ï¿½is kittle oï¿½ steam
Huzzinï¿½ anï¿½ maï¿½zinï¿½ the blessed feï¿½lds wiï¿½ the Divilï¿½s oaï¿½n teï¿½m.
Sin I mun doy I mun doy, thaw loife they says is sweet,
But sin I mun doy I mun doy, for I couldn abeï¿½r to see it.

XVII.
What atta stanninï¿½ theer for, anï¿½ doesn bring ma the aï¿½le?
Doctorï¿½s a ï¿½toï¿½ttler, lass, an aï¿½s hallus iï¿½ the owd taï¿½le;
I weï¿½nt break rules fur Doctor, a knaws naw moor nor a floy;
Git ma my aï¿½le I tell tha, anï¿½ if I mun doy I mun doy.
meaning:
